title: Carrot & Lentil Soup

overview.description: A smooth, lightly spiced soup combining sweet carrots and red lentils with gentle warming spices. Filling, inexpensive and perfect for a weekday lunch or light dinner.

method: Heat the olive oil in a large saucepan over a medium heat.
Add the onion and cook gently for about 8 minutes until softened.
Add the garlic and cook for 1 minute.
Stir in the cumin, coriander and turmeric and cook for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add the carrots and red lentils.
Pour in the stock and bring to the boil.
Reduce to a gentle simmer and cook for about 25 minutes until the carrots and lentils are completely tender.
Stir in the double cream if using and heat through gently.
Season to taste with salt and freshly ground black pepper.
Serve with a sprinkling of chopped coriander if desired.

learning.did-you-know: Red lentils break down completely during cooking, making them perfect for naturally thickening soups without needing flour or cream.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How gently toasting ground spices develops their flavour, how lentils create a silky texture, and how to balance sweetness with savoury seasoning.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: An inexpensive pantry-friendly soup that's nutritious, freezer-friendly and ideal for busy weekdays. The gentle spices add interest without overpowering the vegetables.
chapman-tips: Rinse the lentils before cooking to remove excess starch.
The soup should taste gently spiced rather than like a curry.
A squeeze of lemon juice just before serving brightens the flavours.
notes.batch-cooking: Freeze in family-sized portions for up to three months.
notes.leftovers: Reheat gently with a splash of stock if the soup thickens during storage.
